Take 2: we used an immersion blender in the Dutch oven prior to adding the kale, as the thickening method instead of smashing some or the beans. Huge improvement in texture and flavor.

I’d also argue for adding one more seasoning component. We used turmeric the second time, which was nice. Ginger, red pepper, flakes, etc could also work. Just needs something else.

Don’t skip on the grated parm or lemon at the end, either!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This was ok.  A bit bland even with extra salt, pepper and crushed red pepper.  I would cut the chicken broth in half - way too soupy.  May add more garlic and herbs.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I added a whole handful of Italian seasonings and a healthy dash of cayenne but otherwise followed the recipe, and it was a winner! Husband has already asked for this to enter our regular dinner rotation.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I love this recipe. Quick and easy and simple and healthy and flavorful. I did add extra pepper and Kräutersalz at the end for more depth of flavor and I agree with the comments about browning the chicken a bit before adding the liquids, but overall love it. Checks all my boxes for a recipe.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Used rotisserie chicken (but shred and add at the end!) and a squeeze of lemon juice. Amazing dish.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Prepared as instructed. Delicious and easy. I added extra beans and kale. Next time maybe some carrots.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Used spinach because it was on hand. Easy recipe to follow.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Added some tomato paste to the sauté before adding liquid 😍 yum

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I added a Parmesan rind during the simmer and finished each bowl with lemon juice before serving. Easy and delicious!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This was a pretty bland stew so I added the lemon zest and juice as well as Aleppo pepper and Parmesan. It was very quick and easy to make and nutritious. We’re used to more flavor packed recipes. This was fine, a little doctoring up to taste is needed though.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Great basic recipe with plenty of opportunity to add your own influence.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! My husband is a big eater so I added some orzo and a little more broth to fill it out a little more. Very lovely and easy soup!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This is a super simple recipe that is delicious and healthy. I read all these notes and agree with: browning both sides of the breasts before removing them and continuing with the recipe. The opportunity to add umami from their caramelization should not be squandered. I increased the amount of beans (pureed almost a can's worth to thicken the liquid) and chicken stock to create more of a soup. I used dried sage from my garden and left it in.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I used only 1 can Great Northern beans; I used spinach; chicken thighs (cut into pc and browned a little); more onion and ... 2 big handfuls of sauteed chopped mushrooms. great.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: easy and delicious - and can be tweaked and adapted to what you have in the fridge easily. will cook again and again,

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Delicious after trial and error of adding many different seasonings. Be warned that the end result is very bland as written. I’ll leave it up to the individual to choose a seasoning theme.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Living out west with a charcoal grill close at hand I always grill the chicken with lots of smoky wood before shredding. Really ups the flavor of the dish in my opinion.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Lemon squeeze.  Garnish with parm.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: sub chicken thighs, added chili flakes, Orzo, parsley, dill and lemon. The beans, fat from the chicken, and butter thickened it up nicely. very tasty and simple.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Satisfying and delicious. I adapted the recipe by substituting chicken sausage, which I grill and add at the finish. This makes it even more rustic. Great with a squeeze or lemon for brightness, or soy sauce for umami. And topped with Parmesan.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Very easy and was satisfying on one of the coldest days, but overall I would say it’s good but not great.  There are better soup recipes that I would go with before this one.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I went a little rogue on this and it is fabulous! I roasted some bone-in breasts, then made stock with the bones in the instant pot. Also made beans in the IP instead of canned. Browned garlic, rosemary and red pepper flakes then threw in the shredded meat, beans and frozen kale with the stock. Omg. It took three hours of mostly hands off work, but came together really quick at the end.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This was spectacular with modifications. I dry marinated chicken cutlet strips (that was what I had on hand) in 1/4 tsp each paprika, coriander, and salt, seared it in a tbsp of olive oil, took out, deglazed the pan with a tiny bit of balsamic and red wine, then added half the butter and sauteed the onions and garlic. I also doubled the rosemary and used one less cup of broth. Added more salt andanother half tsp of the same spices while simmering. Otherwise made as directed and served with rice.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This experienced cook has been enjoying expressing creativity when cooking by utilizing ingredients on hand. I also browned the chicken first but did not cook through. Then I added it later to acquire flavor from the broth mixture. I substituted thyme for sage or rosemary and finely chopped broccoli for greens.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody:
This dish is high on the effort: reward ratio. simple ingredients, everything to make it was in my pantry, frig  or freezer. served with crusty bread. perfect warm hearty dinner for freezing night! 
I used fresh rosemary, sage and thyme because I had them.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: So tasty, so easy. Followed the recipe, but added a little turmeric for a healthy boost. Quick and yummy dinner, perfect for a cold night.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Have leftover rotisserie chicken.  Could I use this to save some more time?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: @AVLDot Don’t see why not!  I’m planning to use the remains of a roast chicken I cooked and deboned yesterday!  Waste not, want not, as the saying goes!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I used (defrosted) shredded chicken from my freezer.  I'm sure I lost a little flavor but it made this a really quick dinner; sauteed the aromatics, added a little turmeric and black pepper before adding the broth.  Simmered the beans for a few minutes then added the chicken and spinach.  Heated through until the spinach wilted.  Will definitely keep in the rotation when I need a quick but warming meal.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This recipe goes in my 5 star recipe collection. Used Better Than Boullion for the broth, large bunch of lacinato kale, more chicken, more garlic and the suggested parm rind. High protein, high fiber, great taste.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Rotisserie chicken and kale salad mix from costco. Canned beans? That's where I draw the line! Gotta be dried Peruvian beans, cooked with a ham hock sometimes.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This is just like the Pasta Fagiola recipe my family has made for decades just with escarole, garlic, and parm. It is truly a keeper! I grew up with the addition of oregano, parsley, and tomato juice/sauce but my husband and daughter are allergic to tomato, so just the chicken broth now. We sometimes add in rotisserie chicken for protein, but prefer ground Italian sausage for more of a kick. Any of these options are easy, fast, and delicious. Try it!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Definitely use thighs instead of breast!!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This really worked out great! A very healthy and comforting dish that I made by cooking 3 parts separately (roasted turkey thigh, soaked and cooked gently white beans cannelloni, and the the onion garlic butter sautéed with 12 oz thawed frozen spinach) all three combined mixed and coated with1/2 cup finely grated parmigiana with just chicken stock.  The roasted turkey thigh is a game changer!lean, tasty if seasoned for a few hours beforehand and then roasted…and then sliced up and mixed in with the other two dishes.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Agreed on the need for some more flavor- so along with mushrooms, I added more garlic, 2 slabs of ginger root, fresh cilantro, extra rosemary, Thai basil (growing thru the winter inside) lemon juice, white wine and some chili peppers. Added fresh peas just before serving over jasmine rice.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Received in my inbox today, made it tonight for dinner. Very good, easy chicken soup/stew. Best thing is not needing to precook the chicken! Made as written with the following changes as noted in other reviews: Used sage instead of rosemary, spinach as greens, 2 cans whole beans, 1 can smashed, added 2 sliced carrots with the onion, at the end added juice of a half of a lemon, 1/2 cup or so of heavy cream, and some fresh grated nutmeg, and plenty of fresh pepper. Delish!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I cooked the kale in a bit of broth and olive oil and lime juice before adding it to the pot. Then because its texture was too chewy and fibrous I whizzed the kale in a food processor and added it back to the pot. I added a half cup of white wine to enhance the flavour.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody:
I followed this recipe closely but made numerous modifications and it was so good! 
1. Used double the garlic
2. Doubled the broth amount + I had an open vegetable broth container so used it plus a low sodium chicken broth
3. Whole Foods offers a fully seasoned, whole rotisserie chicken for $5.99 in Tuesdays. Pulled the meat, chopped it up, added the meat, bones and skin. 
4. Added carrots and cooked them when sautéing the onions, etc.
5. Used red Swiss Chard
 
Finished cooking in a covered large pot for a couple of hours in the low setting. Just wow!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: When you add the beans do you include the liquid in the can?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: As another reader commented this is quite a bland recipe which needs a lot to lift it.  Parmesan, lemon juice/white wine, ginger and olive oil are all essential.  Chicken breast was very dry - would use bone in chicken thigh next time and cook slower and longer.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This is on the monthly menu in our house. I pick up a rotisserie chicken from BJ's for $4.99 which makes it even easier.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Found this recipe after a notification from NYT. Read all the comments and made it tonight. A big hit for hubby and me!  I made the full recipe. Two changes: sautéed the chicken first (seasoned it well ahead of time, used Penzy’s Justice spice). And added frozen corn (just because I needed to use it up). Used all the broth as recipe called for.  I used fresh collard greens and also added one carrot thinly sliced. Absolutely recommend this recipe; it’s highly forgiving for customization.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Ok- absolutely NOTHING bland about this. Ya gotta develop the onion and garlic w/ rosemary sprig. I added red pepper. I did brown the chicken first- but thanks NYT for teaching me how to GENTLY poach chicken. It’s delicious. I added lemon juice and grated parm at end because other folks suggested parm rind and I forgot… it’s seriously delicious- and I don’t like soup!!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: So delicious and easy...I modified this based on what I had in the refrigerator and pantry. I substituted the kale with 1 fennel bulb and the tender tops; used shallots and green onions instead of the yellow onion, I used 4 ounces of roasted chicken from the freezer. Then I added 1 tbsp. of dried crushed New Mexico green Chile. A superb variation on an excellent, and easy recipe.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This is a simple recipe but oh so good! We’re in an ice storm, so I used what was on hand: rotisserie chicken, basil for greens. Per the comments, I added a parm rind. And because we like spicy, also added a little Calabrian chili paste. Spectacular flavor, and warmth down to the toes. Thanks, Ali!!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Was excellent with butter beans and spinach

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: What is a "sprig" of sage?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Very good comfort food for a cold winter night.  Loved the texture from the kale, beans, and chicken, although it was a little bland.  I always make recipes as is the first time, but next time we will try some of the suggestions from the comments.  Will definitely make again.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Super easy to make, and just plain delicious.  Made exactly as written.  Leftovers were great too!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Super yummy and quick to develop rich taste unlike most stews/soups I've made.  Chopped up collard green leaves and stems for the green leafy veg.  One change:  added collard green stems and some chopped carrots into the sautéed onions. The carrots provided nice color. Highly recommend this recipe.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I made the recipe as written, but found it a bit lacking in flavor. I'll do it again, but I may use chicken thighs instead of breasts, and I'll definitely brown the meat first, along with the onions and herbs. I used chard instead of kale, since that's what I had. I might also add a bit of paprika or hot sauce to give it a bit of zing. This would also be good with a bit of sausage, in addition to, or instead of, the chicken. It needs a little wakeup, e.g., hot pepper flakes or pepper sauce.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody:
Solid satisfying simple meal. A bit like a cozy chicken pot pie, without the pastry (and soupier). Easy template to add what you have or want. Followed all steps and ingredients, along with these ingredient additions or changes: 
* Added a chopped fennel bulb, sliced mushrooms and cubed red potatoes to the onions and garlic. 
* used boneless skinless chicken thighs 
* 3 not 4 cups broth (plenty soupy) 
*added a spoonful of mild harissa along with the broth 
*used baby spinach for the greens 
* added 1 cup frozen peas in the last minute 
*salt, pepper, red pepper flakes at end
Solid4-star homey dinner—not dazzling but so good. 
M

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: An easy,good tasting meal. I followed the directions with two additions mentioned by others. I added small carrot slices to the onion mixture and used 3 cans of beans. This will definitely be a meal repeated.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody:
The flavor , for us was just fine . The end result  seemed too thin. We ended up adding four cans of beans and spinach as the greens.
Only issue was the thinness. Would definitely make it again .

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and healthy. I had a cooked chicken and its flavorful broth which made this recipe simple and easy.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Do not substitute ground turkey. It simply doesn't have enough flavor. Loved it with the ground pork, though.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Lovely recipe.  I didn't add any salt or pepper - the garlic and the herbs (I used dried thyme, garam masala and rosemary) give enough flavour.  Next time I would use less kale, not an entire bunch - a whole bunch is a bit too much.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: So much flavor even without searing the chicken! Perfect weeknight, cozy meal as advertised.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Loved this!  Great chilly weeknight meal that takes no time at all.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Used pinches of dried sage and rosemary instead of fresh sprigs. Also added a pinch of red pepper flakes and squeeze of lemon. Would make again.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Not worth the minimum effort required. Have deleted this from my saved recipes, as I don't want to be disappointed again. Perhaps it's the chicken breasts in Texas. Perhaps it's the greens. Maybe it's my lack of skill. This is the first time in years I've used chicken breasts, and now I know why. Give me a whole chicken or thighs.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Cooked as written. Fantastic and simple. I added a squirt of lemon and some feta to my bowl. Delicious.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I added a can of corn (NOT creamed) (I'm a corn-aholic). Yum!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: followed the recipe and some of the comments: added carrot, lemon, parmesan rind, and ginger. Fed both of us with enough for another meal. Perfect dish for the polar vortex, and maybe one of the most delicious NYT dishes I've ever made.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. Off the heat I added lemon and parmesan with a side of crusty bread. I'll be making this again

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Made as directed and did try topping with some fresh parmasean, which was good. Next time I would like to try adding some white wine to brighten. Pancetta bits might also be interesting to add.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Made as written. The broth was surprisingly very buttery. We added lemon and ate on toast.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Made this last night and it was a hit! I used more broth than the recipe called for because my cans of beans were 19 oz instead of 15, and that made it a little more soupy than I'd intended. Removed some broth and beans and blended them to thicken it up, and at the last minute I threw in some farro to soak up more liquid. The lemon juice was a nice touch at the end. My teenaged sons gobbled it up. Next time I'll double the recipe for leftovers!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Have to say the chicken gets leathery and tough, especially when you reheat. Maybe cutting it in chunks instead of shredding it might help??

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Really bland!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: A KEEPER!  Made pretty much per instructions.  Rosemary sprig, always add extra garlic and always rinse the beans to help reduce sodium along with low sodium chicken broth. Used spinach.  Seems like it needed something...would definitely add even more garlic, maybe double?  And an extra can of beans wouldn't hurt as most got mashed.  We loved it and fairly quick to put together.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Just wanted to add that it was even better the next evening!  Flavors had more chance to meld. Spinach worked just fine.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Spinach would be a little delicate for this recipe. Kale is an excellent choice

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody:
Had bone in, skin on chicken thighs.  Pulled the skin off and put it in the oven to make chicken skin crisps while the thighs poached in the liquid. Also used up some frozen kale.  Otherwise, followed the recipe as is. Topped the bowls with crumbled chicken skin and this was a satisfying and delicious dinner.

I think I'll try this next time with ground chicken (browning it in step 1) to not have to shred the chicken.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody:
I served this as a soup with salad, just didn't cream so many beans. I'll probably add some stock to the leftovers unless serving over rice.
Good recipe overall, I didn't get a lot of flavor from the rosemary, so I may have to up that or leave it in the sauce for a bit.
I love things that finish with 'season with salt and pepper' because I can let the diners do that.
I will definitely make this again.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Loved this but made some changes along the way. Had 1.25 pounds of chicken so added 1 extra cup of water. I added a third can of cannellini beans for body (which helped tremendously).  To amp up the flavor, I used both dried ground sage and rosemary, added some onion powder and better than bullion.  My only rookie error was using ghee instead of butter as someone had mentioned.  The flavor of ghee is too strong for this stew, so use butter. It also took longer than 15 minutes to get the chicken up to temp. Will make it again!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Love this. So easy. I used Veg broth (better than B) and threw in a parmesan rind (thanks Joe Pro). Great for the below freezing weather right now.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I only added more chicken stock and a squeeze of lemon at the end.  This is such a keeper!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Cooked last night -- will be putting this to the rotation as it's pretty easy. Added about a cup of cubed butternut that we needed to use. Increased the garlic by half, smashed them good but didn't cut. We also had some cooked small shell pasta, so I added a bit to the bowls before serving. Pasta was nice but not needed. Threw in some red pepper flake, could have added a bit more to kick up the interest. Served with sliced baguette. Husband does not like beans but got two helpings!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Added a bit of fresh ginger, finely chopped, to the onion and garlic.😋👍

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Made a double batch. Was enough for dinner for three adults two nights in a row, had enough left over for three to four additional servings. Added parmesan rind after adding the broth as mentioned in previous posts, highly recommend. Seasoned the chicken with Tony Chachere's, yellow curry powder, Italian seasoning, and a drizzle of Olive Garden Italian Dressing. Sauteed the chicken first then followed the recipe mostly as written. Served with both sourdough bread and naan bread as I had both on hand, equally delicious options. Overall solid recipe with a few tweaks for enhanced flavor. Will be added to the regular rotation especially for winter time!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Perfect receipt made as is!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I made with spinach and I added diced tomatoes. The chicken was perfectly cooked but bland - I agree with Greenie, I would season and saute the chicken next time before adding it into the liquid.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This stew is what you should see next to “comfort food” in the dictionary. Highly recommend punching up the seasonings — curry powder, harissa, crushed red pepper, whatever you like — but it’s a good, hearty dish for winter.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I enjoyed this, Ali. Thank you!! We had Costco pulled chicken on hand instead of cooking the breasts in the broth. I took some of the comments here and added a can of corn, about half drained, and about 2 tsp of turmeric for color and taste. Used the rest of a tub of spinach, at least what I could salvage before compost, and it was a delicious stew! I forgot to mash the beans, but I will next time.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I used chicken thighs (in chunks instead of shredded) instead of Breast, add herb de Provence and mixed in both spinach and kale.  It turned out beautiful and I will be putting this on rotation.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I used corn-fed chicken (the one with yellow meat), a good stock concentrate (Maggi, it's the consistency of loose pudding, in small jars, for those in Europe), and 3 sprigs (with branches) of sage - it tasted phenomenal and phenomenally chickeny! Definitely a keeper and will make again.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Prefect cozy all in one meal. Subbed in Turkish oregano for sage/rosemary (didn’t have on hand), and sautéed a celery rib with the onion and garlic because I had it lying around. A big squeeze of lemon to finish adds the right touch.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: A pefect recipe!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Very good and easy to make! I ended up using a rotisserie chicken, doubling the garlic, adding tomato paste to the garlic and onions, and juice from half a lemon at the end. Even my toddler loved it and asked for more!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: If I have cooked chicken breast meat, could i add it later in the recpe?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: @Pumpkin Eater I used rotisserie chicken and put it in at the end.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: How do you prepare the chicken for this recipe?  It goes in raw?  Is it sliced into strips or some other form?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: One Whole Foods organic chicken yields 1 pound of meat. I used spinach instead of kale, and added carrot and celery to increase the vegetables. The servings are huge. Added all together, one serving is between three and four cups.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: So good and easy!  Also found it to be a great use of the chicken breast tenders my husband mistakenly picked up instead of regular chicken breasts.  Using the tenders solved the timing issue other commenters had noted re: chicken breasts needing additional time to cook, and the tenders shredded beautifully. Used frozen spinach for the greens.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: We made it almost as is, had rotisserie chicken so shredded that and used dried rosemary...very tasty and we ate it with parmesan rosemary artisan bread :) Perfect for a cold snowy icy day!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Added a good-sized parm rind and red pepper flakes with the stock. Juice of half a lemon before serving. Would have been bland without!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody:
So, used frozen chard from garden, cannellini beans and chicken breast.
Sautéed the onion and spices - used 1 t of cumin, adobo and Florida seasoned pepper- in my instant pot. Added and sautéed the chicken, then added the beans, chard and chicken broth. Switched off sautee and hit the soup button. Absolutely delish!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This is very good but does need changes made in comments. Brown the chicken (I used boneless thighs, too), added red pepper flakes, more garlic. Drizzled a bit of wild mushroom/sage infused EVOO on top.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Thanks for the tips on adding palm rind and a little lemon juice and red pepper chili flakes. I am using lean chicken sausage instead of the chicken breast. It smells divine. Also blended 3/4 of a cup of cannellini beans with some of the broth and will add it at the end for creaminess.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: It was great I put the beans in a little later because I thought they would get soggy and they were great!!!!!!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: It was great both my kids loved it but there was not a lot of beans two cans was not enough but it was still pretty good

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: What we made was awful! Must be the chicken which was chewy. We chose to put cannellini beans. Not sure what we did wrong… We won’t do it again!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Is 5his a soup or a stew?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Used tarragon as that's what I had in the fridge. Delicious!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Made a half recipe which was perfect for my husband and me. Used parts of a rotisserie chicken- bones for making the 2 cups of stock and the meat for the soup. In lieu of fresh herbs used some dried turkey rub which had sage and rosemary in it. For greens used lacinato kale. We agreed it was an incredibly delicious soup- 100% make again!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Wow! This was so easy and absolutely fantastic! I don’t understand the comments that it lacked flavor…I used lacinto kale and rosemary. I also added gnocchi at the end (about half of a store-bought package). A squeeze of lemon and Parmesan when served is a must!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: A very tasty, healthy & quick one pot stew. I had some chicken breast tenders & some red Swiss kale in the fridge. My hubby was fighting a bad cold so I was looking for a new soup or stew to try when I found this perfect recipe. The only change I made was to add the zest of one lemon.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Rotisserie chicken, sweet onion, fresh flat parsley(no others greens in the fridge)   Absolutely yummy. Will make again.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Family agreed one of the best chicken soup recipes we've had!  I did make a few tweaks as suggested by others (doubled herbs 2 rosemary and 2 sage, seared chicken first and deglazed pan, had a 3rd can of beans that I blended with broth and added back in to thicken the soup, finished with half a squeezed lemon and drizzled some truffle oil on top and fresh shaved parm, added baby spinach to the bowls and added soup on top so it wilted better).  Simple, and my house smelled amazing!!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Sauté the chicken with the onions is a very good suggestion.   The recipe is a bit bland without that step    Or maybe I just need to add more salt.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Would adding ginger work?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Making for the 2nd time today. I used a bag of Rancho Gordo beans(can’t remember the name, but a white that was suggested for soups/stews) and soaked overnight. Used thyme sprigs because that’s what I had. I added the beans plus soaking water. Added a couple of carrots, a fennel branch removed later, Manchego rind and Lemon. So good and EASY👏

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Made as written and was so surprised at how flavorful it was (for such a quick, simple recipe). I made it again and subbed some roasted tofu for the chicken. I thought it was equally good. I’m making it again tomorrow and will revert to the original recipe. It needs no alterations.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Added  a bit of dried thyme and red pepper flakes.  low on kale so put in some broccoli and mushrooms.  and some leftover brown rice.   it was good!  chicken only needed 10 min.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Super easy and delicious. I halved-ish the recipe but added extra spinach and some leftover mushrooms I had. I could see adding some white wine to it, but I didn’t feel like opening a bottle. Overall quite yummy and healthy!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this twice in the last ten days.  Both times I 1) added a huge handful of chopped carrots with the onion/garlic/herb step 2) used the meat from a rotisserie chicken 3) used 5 cups of bone broth and 4) added 1/4+ cup dry basmati rice during the simmer step for a little more heft.  Once I used spinachas my green of choice; this time I used kale.  It’s not the world’s most exciting soup but it’s a great little recipe if you’re feeling under the weather and don’t want to work too hard.  And the leftovers are so, so good.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Used some leftover roasted turkey and that was quite good!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Adding a good size pinch of “Italian herbs,” and then adding balsamic vinegar to serving bowl added a very welcome sensation of acidity and depth of flavor.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Do I rinse the beans?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody:
The general rule is: unless a recipe tells you to do something, you don't.  On the other hand, I've seen several recipes on here using canned bean go out of their way to affirmatively tell you to add the the liquid with the beans - as if they expected you to rinse, otherwise.  So the answer is...

NYTimes Cooking's instructions are not consistent on the matter of canned beans, and you are not likely to get an answer from them.

But I would rinse them.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I used ground rosemary and spinach for the greens because it's what I had.  I think kale would hold better and add more chew.  The taste was great but I think I would not cook the beans quite so much as they just fell apart and became mush.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I made it with chickpeas and it was awesome! Very flavorful!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I followed some commenters' suggestions - namely used plenty of salt and pepper, used spinach, and mashed a whole extra can of beans into the soup. I also added some powdered rosemary and sage, as well as Aleppo pepper and a splash of lemon juice. Yet we found it to be very lacking in flavor. I like the idea of this soup, so I think I'll give it another shot, but will add Hatch green chile powder to see if that gives it a little oomph.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This soup was absolutely delicious! I used half a chicken just under 2 lbs., 2 Adobo chilies (the heat was just right) and 1 can of beans. The cilantro and avocado are a must. The leftovers are fantastic too.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Rainbow Chard - delicious. Add one extra can of beans.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Added mushrooms and carrots; swapped out kale for spinach. I used smaller chicken breasts, so I should have cooked a couple minutes less to keep them more tender. But this recipe is as easy as it is healthy and tasty!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: @Mary Fox I would soak the beans overnight and cook them for an hour or two until tender. Then proceed with the recipe.  Remember that you don't want to add salt while the beans are cooking (makes them tough) so you will need to add a lot more salt to the stew.  I use my Rancho Gordo beans like this all the time.  I do add herbs while they are first cooking.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and easy to make!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Made a half portion with home made chicken stock and fresh rosemary.  Had no fresh greens so used a lovely zucchini and, as commenters suggested, a carrot.  Simmered a bit longer.  Was delicious and seems to be highly flexible!  Will definitely make again!!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I used spinach (and some leftover iceberg salad and tomatoes) instead of kale, added a can of chickpeas, 200 grams of farfalle and some heavy cream, and seasoned it with sambal oelek.  Amazingly good, given that I was only looking for a way to use up some on-sale chicken breasts and a bunch of wilting spinach ... My teenagers finished the pot in a day.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: There is no chicken shown in the ingredients.  Looks like thighs in the photo. How much or how many thighs and how to cook?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: @Sharon S it literally says a pound of chicken breast

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Like the combination of chicken, beans and kale a lot. Cooked chicken breast came out hard and dry for me so would highly recommend thighs instead. Also will add more beans when I give it another try.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I love this one! I used more chicken stock and two bunches of kale, along with minced shallots and leeks to add more veg. I also minced fresh rosemary and thyme and put it straight in the pot. I like a strong herb note. I also used an organic rotisserie chicken. When time to serve I dished it with lots of lemon zest and aged parm! Low effort when you're tired!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this twice - the first time was boring. Second time delish. I used olive oil, tbl spoon of rosemary and savory, and for the broth used a good paste and one of the cups from from the beans. I also cooked the chicken separately with garlic salt on them because I needed to cook three pounds up. The result is delicious! Will freeze some for another day.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Made this as written and it was a little soupier than I wanted, adding some orzo solved that! Lemon zest & juice and chili flakes is to finish, lovely!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Very easy and versatile recipe. I made it as written except I had to substitute fresh thyme as I had that on hand. Also did a light pass through with my immersion blender before I added chicken (rotisserie). All my picky eaters enjoyed it. Delicious and simple.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Love this.  So easy to make.  I might add more beans and greens, but still great as is.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Really benefits from red pepper flake, splash of red wine vinegar and parm

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Oh my people! Delicious, second time cooking this. I browned boneless skinless thighs, skipped the Kale, and instead added frozen carrots and broccoli. The lemon is a must.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: it's the rosemary that makes this dish, so use a large sprig . This solid recipe is even BETTER the next day!  Adding Parmesan the first night helped it to bulk up a bit, but not necessary the next day. I used small white beans which was not a good idea. I think the larger ones would've made this much better. Freezes really well.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Almost 2 feet of snow outside and this was the perfect dish to warm the soul.  I didn't have white beans, so I used chick peas instead and it was delicious.  I browned the chicken first and added some lemon juice at the end.  I will definitely serve this in the regular rotation.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This is a just a kind of soup that really consistently works for me. The garlic, onion, and sage butter made my whole house smell delicious and then it got to be the base for the whole broth. I will say that since the garlic is smashed rather than minced it didn't really cook down into the broth, but that was no problem at all for me. Chicken got nice and moist and pearly, I simmered the whole thing on medium for a few minutes after shredding the chicken to set my salmonella paranoia to rest.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I liked the idea of this but 4 cups of broth seemed like a lot. I only used 2, simmered without a cover, and it was still very, very soupy. I wouldn't use more than one cup next time.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Great base recipe. Modifications were: used thighs, sautéed them with the onions, added some red pepper flakes and the parm rind, and ended with the juice of a lemon. Really delicious and going in the rotation.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody:
It was delicious! Really enjoyed the textures and flavours - we used sage and rosemary from the garden, as well as a zucchini - large cubes. It felt like a Chicken of the Provence :)

Thank you from Australia!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I made two minor mistakes: added a parmesan rind without cutting back on added salt, and browned the chicken first but didn't cut the simmering time so it overcooked a bit. I won't bother browning next time. The stew is delicious, and the lemon adds a perfect note.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Agree that it needs a lot more seasoning. I used leftover liquid from a jar of kimchi to add some punch.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Second time making this. Came back for more but this time used some lean ground pork that I had in fridge can of garbanzos and can of white kidneys and then added chilli pepper flakes to spice it up a touch and a ton of leafy green spinach. Simple and warm. Great for a fall/winter / early spring day. Super simple and easy too.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This soup was just okay. It was a little bland and a little uninspiring. The chicken didn’t add much, and omitting it would make the recipe very affordable.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I would add more chicken broth so it is more soupy.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: A friend had made it and recommended both dark and white meat for added flavor and I have now made this twice that way. I found that by the time the soup is at a simmer, the chicken is done! I used dry rosemary and one sprig of sage, and in addition to the kale, I added spinach, since I like the taste of it.Very good recipe

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Chiffonade of basil to step one. Also carrots and mushrooms

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Healthy, easy; delicious. This ticks all my boxes.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Simple and delicious. I used a box of baby kale for my greens and included a parm rind in Step 2 and took it out with the chicken in Step 3.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Added the mushrooms and Parmesan rind as others suggested.  Swapped in one can of red kidney beans for one of the cans of cannellini beans.  Otherwise cooked as is and it was delicious!  I would definitely make again.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Add park rind

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I made the recipe as is, and came back to it a second time and cut up the chicken in long pieces. It is a bit quicker and tastes the same. Love this quick and healthy recipe.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Ideal recipe because I had everything in the house. Easy.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Used the 1/2 function because I had only one chicken breast. A leek in my fridge that needed to be used; supplemented with a bit of frozen chopped onion. Appreciated the suggestion to add a parmesan rind. Tossed in some brown rice late in the game with a small dash of red pepper flakes. Squeeze of lemon. So good. Might try 1 anchovy at the start next time to see where that leads. There will be a next time for sure!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Made it last night and it was a quick and easy dinner.  Used some leftover rice in the bottom of the bowl and put the stew on top.  Perfect quick dinner.  Would add a bit of parmesan on top next time but, that would be my only change.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I think this is excellent as written.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Made this with escarole, only subs were poultry seasoning instead of sage sprigs and I went easy on the garlic. It was a 10/10!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: unbelievably good! i added some carrot for more veg, used both sage and rosemary, and added a bunch of fresh dill at the end. SO delicious

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Easy and delicious. Include the juice of a lemon and some red pepper flakes, you won’t regret it.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: We really liked this.  I used kale and rosemary.  I think next time I will up it to 3 cans of beans, but it was a delicious meal.  Served it with some brown and serve hot rolls.  Yum.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This is a beautiful recipe! I was surprised how good it was because it’s so simple and easy. The sage is lovely. Low effort, very high payoff!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I use my stand mixer with the paddle attachment to pull the chicken, it's so much faster and easier than trying to do it with forks. Try it, it's a miracle! Just be aware you may have to hold the thing down a little, as this is pretty tough work for the mixer. And you'll have to clean up some splatter. But this trick has been a game changer.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Keeper.  Of course, used thighs.  Went with rosemary, but would add fried sage leaves for character.  Had a hunk of Parmesan rind that made it. Bread, crusty, was an awesome addition.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: My husband and I work nights so I’m always looking for great reheat and eat recipes. This one is great, especially on a cold, rainy night. I had some leftovers from the NYT Garlicky Chicken with Lemon-Anchovy Sauce recipe that I also threw in and it worked perfectly!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: For how simple this recipe is, I was a little suspect. But it really did work for me! Obviously it’s not complex, but it does taste like a fancier good old fashioned chicken soup.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: As written, this recipe needs a lot more liquid. Has anyone else found the same thing?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This is a wonderful recipe.  I cook the chicken separately in the broth and add a splash of white wine.  When the chicken is finished, I remove it and then add the beans and kale.  I also add chopped carrots for some additional consistency.  Delicious.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody:
I combined this recipe with the NYT Creamy Braised White Beans Recipe. I tweaked both recipes a bit, but not by much. After letting the milk simmer in the chicken peas, I poured the entire thing into this recipe and let them simmer as one for about 20-30 minutes.
A major winner1

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Delish!  Do not skip the lemon!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Yum! We made it according to the recipe the first time and it was okay but felt a little lacking. Today we made it again but added three medium potatoes, extra rosemary, four bay leaves, and lots of pecorino romano—it was so much better!!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I added garam masala for more flavor with just a teaspoon or so of honey to round out the flavor.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Was very good, but next time I'll make it with thighs. The breasts were a little bland and easy to overcook.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Very easy to cook. Per comments, I added lemon juice. Works for me.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Used more broth to make it soupier, and served over white rice.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I made Melissa Clark's Instant Pot hummus for lunch yesterday, and used the chickpea cooking water in place of chicken stock for this recipe last night. It was great!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I used spinach instead of Kale too and fresh basil vs. rosemary. I put fresh lemon juice in it too. I used too much chicken in the end; will cut back next time and add a third can of beans. Think that would make it perfect!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Great easy stew like dish. Good with any garden herbs. First time I made it with thyme and it was delicious.  Serves more than 4 if you make it with a side dish or yam or whatever you like. I finished it off with some smoked paprika. This dish is a keeper.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Found this while searching for recipes to use some leftover grilled chicken.  Since the chicken was cooked, I shredded it and added it last.  Also found the base a little bland, so I sliced and lightly sauted one link of andouille sausage and put sausage and brown bits in during step 3. It was delicious.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: So lovely. Added a squeeze of lemon and topped my bowl with Parmesan and it’s delightful

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: This was a very easy weeknight dinner and SO good. I’ve never used escarole before but it is easy and a mild green. High fiber and protein a definite make-again. I served with parm on the side to top it, and used bone broth in the cooking.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Made as is and was very good. Next time I added some penne pasta leftover from a box and added it with beans. This was also very good. Round three will be Italian sausage subbed for chicken.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this at least 5 times and haven’t been able to get the chicken to have an appetizing texture. I’ve tried different brands of chicken and every time it’s so rubbery. Any ideas what I could be doing wrong?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Chicken and White Bean Stew Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and hearty. I definitely use a parmesan rind, chili flakes and extra broth. I prefer chicken thighs as they are more tender and are more forgiving when reheating. Generously squeezing in lemon at the end is a must.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is terrific and quite simple-took me about 10 minutes total. The only thing I changed was draining the tuna, reserving the oil and then using that to dress the salad.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Could I sub spinach for the kale? I’ve never had kale that I enjoyed but am fine with spinach.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: @Cathie I’m going to make it using baby arugula and I think either will be great. I may chop in a little fresh basil and/or parsley to marry flavors and add dimension..

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: @Cathie I was wondering the same thing. Kale is not allowed in my house 🤪

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Cathie,
You may sub anything you want. That's the great thing about cooking. As a kale hater, I share your pain. I usually sub spinach when kale is called for; however, the suggestion of arugula makes my mouth water. Warning...no massaging if it is not kale.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I only had one can of tuna so used that and a can of sardines. Glad I did, it was delicious! Also added some feta.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody:
We had this for dinner tonight and I was pleasantly surprised - I thought it was delicious, filling, and something I’ll make again. The recipe is quick, inexpensive, and so fresh!

We used red onion; tuna in water (we had it on hand) and one bunch of kale. Next time I’d increase the kale by 50% but acknowledge that a “bunch” can vary. A bit of red pepper might be a nice addition.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Hey Cristy! So you can, but don't massage it--it will turn to mush.  Perhaps marinate your onions in the lemon juice and salt while you prep any remaining ingredients, or 10 minutes (there aren't a lot of ingredients to prep!), rubbing the onions between your fingers a couple times to get the massaging effect on them.  If you use red onion it will turn bright pink and that's what you want. Toss with greens to distribute dressing, then toss with remaining.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I thought it was healthy but dull. More lemon? Next time I may add some chopped hard boiled egg, or feta, or toasted pita croutons, or some black olives, or a combination of these.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: "1 large bunch Tuscan kale..." It's always useful to know how much this should be by actual weight. Eight ounces? Twelve ounces?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I steamed my “chopped kale” for about 3 minutes and still did the massage it really turns it from grass to a more salad like experience…

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: @Frann Bragg's sells a product called liquid aminos, find it at any health food store! Great as a dressing

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Great salad to continue healthy January! My only modifications were adding 1 lime to balance flavor, diced jalapeno for a kick and mint.  I love a lemon/lime dressing.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Add amino acids to the kale for a tangier kick. This is a fire, easy, and filling weeknight dinner❤️

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: what is amino acids in the kitchen pantry?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: liquid aminos @Frann

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: @Frann Trader Joe’s sells coconut aminos, also a good substitute for soy sauce for folks who can’t tolerate soy. A tad sweeter than Braggs .

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this with baby arugula and it was absolutely delicious!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I thought this was very tasty and nutritious. The only thing I did different was add some cherry tomatoes for pops of flavour. I agree that some feta would be nice too, but I didn’t add it this time.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is so close to a Marcella Hazan recipe that I love and make often, which happens to even be published at https://cooking.nytimes.com/recipes/5685-bean-and-tuna-salad. It calls for spinach rather than kale - a real pro move IMHO. I'm partial to cooking dried beans this dish, especially RG's Marcella beans.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: @Mike Thanks for the link to the Marcella Hazan recipe. I’m not seeing the spinach (or any greens). Do you simply top the spinach with the tuna, bean and onion mixture or massage the greens first as in this recipe?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I ALWAYS MASSAGE KALE for a salad. I’m amazed that so many restaurants and recipes skip this step—it’s usually the roughness people object to, and massaging removes all that. Kale salads made this way usually last longer than other greens—for days, if you don’t eat it all too fast!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I'm sure I'm in the minority, but I don't like (make that hate) raw onions -- even red ones. Any substitutes you recommend to give it more flavor?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I halved the olive oil, added a dash more lemon juice, and didn’t have mint. I look forward to trying it with mint! Delicious!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: It doesn't mention mint, so, I'm lost.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Not a fan of tuna but I'll definitely try this using sardines instead.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I make a variation of this salad with drained canned tuna, spinach, white beans, and linguini. A little olive oil and parmesan sprinkled on top. Why, that's what I'm making tonight!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: My husband, who does not like kale, thought this salad was great! Super easy and fast to make and very nutritious. I will definitely be making this one time and time again.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Too high in sodium.  Will try using no salt tuna and no salt beans.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Not a kale fan. I used escarole instead. Yummy. Threw in a few black olives and roasted red peppers.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: excellent

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this for lunch. The only variation I made was to add some Kalamata olives. It was delicious and filling. Both of us enjoyed it and we will be making it again.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I love the ingredients in this salad but felt it lacked the rich flavor I was looking for. That said, it absolutely rocked when I put it on a slice of wheat bread with mayonnaise and a pinch of salt.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: @Valerie ... I also hate raw onions, and they make me feel nauseous when I eat them.  However, I love onions that are cooked.  So, for most recipes like this I sauté the onions.  I don't care about the added time required to do that, and the sauteed onions are delicious.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I tried this heated...I sauteed the onions and kale with olive oil, added the beans and then the tuna and tossed with lemon juice right as I was serving - it was FABULOUS!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is perfect, and now that I have learned to massage kale with lemon juice I will do it again and again!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made it exactly as directed, with canned tuna I bought in the Azores. Fabulous! Delicious and satisfying. Interestingly, the juice from a large lemon really softened and tenderized the kale nicely. Easy, healthy, satisfying and delicious. Plus leftovers!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I veganized it by using tofu instead of tuna and use some kelp seasoning to give it some a bit of the flavor. If I had had some nori sheets on hand, I would’ve crumbled those in. It was excellent!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody:
@Erin

I totally missed that this version doesn't serve the beans on a bed of greens. I'm pretty sure it was a serving suggestion in one of the cookbooks. I usually use baby spinach very lightly dressed with EVOO and red wine vinegar.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I loved this salad  - added a handful of golden  raisins to add a little sweetness.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I loved this with a few enhancements: rice vinegar, fish oil, and MSG. Also added thinly sliced cucumber after salting and pressing out excess water. Very satisfying.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: 10 to 12 oz of tuna for one bunch of Kale makes this very tuna forward. I added a second bunch of kale, 3-4 cloves of sliced garlic, and a couple more tablespoons each of lemon juice and oil (which you can get from the tuna, although don't dump the entire contents into the mix). Beefed up the portion to four full servings.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Great simple recipe. Added 1 cup of chopped castelvetrano olives, green onions, basil, and arugula. Used only one can of tuna and no red onion. Excellent

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Use pre-shredded kale and added feta and a bit more lemon juice (juice of a lemon is an ambiguous command to begin with). Very delicious, and very quick and easy.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is excellent, exactly as written.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. Used a shallot as I didn’t have red onion on hand, next time I make this I will double the kale.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Fantastic. Another bop from Ali. This is great with the white onion.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I’ve made this with kale and decided to try with arugula and parsley. Delicious both ways!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is one of my favorite warm-weather meals.  I’ve made a different version of this for years, with abundant parsley instead of the kale. It makes a more protein-heavy version which I often use as a main course in summer. Recently, rather than using canned beans I’ve started using the Rancho Gordo white Lima beans for this recipe. Even though it is slightly more labor intensive, the results are worth it with a hearty, creamy bean that tastes almost like potato.  Also, you can use preserved lemon +/- red chili flakes for an extra punch.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Please clarify, when instructions say “add tuna and oil, toss to combine.” Does that mean add the oil that had been drained off of the tuna? Or do I drain off the tuna, toss out the oil, and add the tuna and whatever oil is left on the pieces? Thanks

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: did not like. sour and mushy.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: I ended up wanting extra dressing. Used a turmeric balsamic. Otherwise a good quick summer dinner.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: White Bean, Tuna and Kale Salad Recipe
commentBody: Added some white miso, lemon zest and herbes de Provence to kick up the flavor a bit.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: This was an excellent, quick and tasty dish. I added an additional clove of garlic because I like garlic. I also added the crushed peppers into the juice and lemon zest prior to adding the noodles, arugula and sardines because my wife is a little adverse to the heat of hot peppers. By adding into the olive oil and lemon juice, I toned the crushed peppers down a little. I also added a little more olive oil as recommended because it was a little dry. I give this two thumbs up.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: I do like the use of pantry ingredients and bold flavors, but I prefer about 2 oz of dry pasta per serving, so would use 1/2 lb for 4 people and double the arugula ( or add another green like parsley, watercress or spinach).

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: I try to make my pasta dishes heavy on sauce and light on pasta, so I use about 1.5 ounces of dry pasta per serving and add a vegetable or salad to the meal. I can't wait to make this, as I have been craving pasta lately. If my sardines are not packed in olive oil, I will simply drain them and add fresh olive oil to the recipe. I really appreciate that this dish has a reasonable amount of fat and sodium.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: We make this with anchovies, which is so delicious much tastier!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: @Linda how many tins of anchovies do you use? I love anchovies but they can be quite salty

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Sorry, not crazy about cheese with the sardines. Take a note from Southern Italians and use olive oil tossed crunchy breadcrumbs. Sardines have sufficient umami for the dish...if you want more, add anchovies. But no cheese, please!!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: @Annabella my rule is always no cheese with fish. We Americans somehow feel that every pasta dish must be finished with grated cheese. Not so.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: It’s nice to see this recipe that is straight out of the pantry and uses Whole Wheat pasta. Given all the articles in the times about heart and brain healthy nutrition that emphasize whole grains I find the dearth of recipes in NYT Cooking that use whole grains really frustrating. I always substitute but cooking time and method alterations are needed. Recipes that incorporate whole grains intentionally would be helpful.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: @AndrewB I heartily concur (no pun intended)! I love the wonderful pasta dishes, but I wish they always at least set forth a whole grain option

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: I've made a nearly identical version with tuna many times, a favorite. Olives and capers are not necessary but a delicious addition. I'm not sure the Italians would approve of the parmesan, but I won't tell.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Great quick meal. I used yellowfin tuna in EVOO and followed the rest of the recipe. Very satisfying and tasty. The lemon is a key ingredient.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Would this be as tasty with tuna?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: @Kim I’m going to make it with tuna this week and report back! I don’t see why it would not be quite good.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: @Kim Try Melissa Clark’s recipe for Spaghetti with TunaCapers and Scallions(and Anchovies.). It’s yummy.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Super fast and terrific. I’ll be making this on a regular basis. I just received a gift box of tinned fish which had sardines with preserved lemons. They were great in this recipe. I wouldn’t hesitate to use any other type of tinned fish.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Elegant and tasty. I put the sardines in the oven on 375 for 20 minutes and they were nice and crispy. Red pepper flakes made a difference and was an unexpected nice touch. I ran out of parm cheese and substituted blue cheese. Delicious.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody:
I made this last night with radiatore instead of spaghetti, and chopped anchovy filets instead of sardines (didn't have any sardines handy, but they're on my shopping list now!).  I threw a poached egg in with the pasta too.  It was absolutely delicious, very much like eating a Caesar salad bowl with little pastas in place of croutons.

For the next time, I would definitely cut back on salt, won't salt the pasta water, and will keep any crunchy topping unsalted.  Not for health reasons, just because the fish, cheese, and pasta tasted plenty salty without adding extra.

Seriously good weeknight dinner though, and it really did come together in under 30 min.  Yum!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Excellent even with pasta that is not whole grain

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: I’ve been looking for the perfect dish to begin incorporating sardines into my diet and this is it!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: This recipe and various versions of it have been a back pocket favorite for years. I have a wildly prolific Meyer lemon tree and almost always have everything on hand...flexible with additions, subtractions and amounts but the garlic, lemon and red pepper are key.  It's still great without arugula if you have none but also works with baby spinach or very thinly shredded kale or chard.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Added extra olive oil. Used garlic confit mashed instead of grated garlic. I used sardines in tomato sauce because that's what I had, and it was delicious.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe!  I added some toasted breadcrumbs on top for a little crunch and texture.  Perfect!!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: I substituted tinned calamari because I didn’t have sardines on hand and it was so tasty!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Thanks for a great recipe, Ali! It was definitely worth buying better sardines from Spain: meaty and not fishy. I added extra arugula but will stay with Ali’s proportion next time. Also surprised at the difference it made to peel rather than grate the Parmesan. I’ll probably use a short pasta next just so I can enjoy it faster!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: So good! My 5 year old also loves it. Omitted the chili flakes. I even like it and don’t typically like sardines. I made it bc my husband and kids do! Less fishy tasting than a tuna dish imo!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Made this last night with no modifications.  It could not have been simpler or more delicious…Surprisingly Delicious!

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Has anyone made a vegetarian version of this?

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: A lot of people fall to realize that boneless skinless sardines are mild and far less fishy tasting than tuna.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe

@Annabella 
 I actually like the Parmesan in there.  I know Italian dishes made with fish or other seafood are generally served without cheese, but plenty of people add cheese anyway.  If you don't like it, feel free to leave it out.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Bravo. I halved everything and it was a perfectly delicious lunch with a nice Verdicchio to wash it down.

author: Ali Slagle
recipeTitle: Spaghetti With Arugula and Sardines Recipe
commentBody: Loved it! I read reviews and pulled in several suggestions from those… I air fried my sardines (which took away so much of the fishy flavor!), added lots of caper and green onions, doubled and chopped up the arugula and it was just fabulous!!! Will absolutely be making again!

author: Ali Slagle

commentBody:
I had some soba noodles instead of the whole wheat pasta - worked really well with a bit of chopped caper leaves and subbed garlic powder instead of grated garlic 
Very delicious and quick!

author: Ali Slagle
commentBody: I like cottage cheese and I am always looking for new and interesting breakfast ideas.  This fit the bill nicely.  Fast and tasty.  Good effort to reward ratio!  I will be putting this into the breakfast rotation.

author: Ali Slagle
commentBody: I just tried this. Now, my recent go-to breakfast has been a concoction my suster has dubbed "Apple Vomit" which is cottage cheese, peanut butter, a touch of honey or maple syrup,  and some cinnamon or pumpkin pie spice,  so this is just a variation on that theme. I used a raw tahini (the sesame seeds are not toasted) and maple syrup because I'm out of honey, and topped it with banana. It scales down beautifully to one serving, and it is tasty!

author: Ali Slagle
commentBody:
@E. Parker
Sisters! Gotta love their frank and saucy comments.

author: Ali Slagle
commentBody: Really tasty quick recipe. If anyone is in the UK, I cannot recommend the Longley Farm cottage cheese brand enough!

author: Ali Slagle
commentBody: @GG It’s truly life changing. Not much of a cottage cheese eater but stumbled on it while on vacation. It’s like discovering French cheeses after only eating velveeta. You realize what a poor product we’ve settled for here in the US, all salt and gloopy. And I’m including good culture in that group of subpar too. British and Polish cottage cheese is transformative.

author: Ali Slagle
commentBody: This really elevated my usual mundane cottage cheese breakfast. I’m definitely adding this to my breakfast options.

author: Ali Slagle
